title: hypochaeris radicata subsp. heterocarpa
intro: Hypochaeris radicata subsp. heterocarpa, also known as the hairy cat's ear or common cat's ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues. The leaves and roots of Hypochaeris radicata subsp. heterocarpa are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
